description:
You are a biomedical engineer in a hospital 3D printing lab preparing a surgical case package for tomorrow's neurosurgical case conference. The surgical team requires multi-tissue 3D models, cranial measurements, and documentation views from the CT Cranium DICOM series already loaded in InVesalius 3. Detailed specifications are at /home/ga/Documents/case_conference/case_specs.txt.

Segmentation: Create a "Cortical Bone" mask (662-1988 HU), a "Full Bone" mask (226-3071 HU), and a "Soft Tissue" mask (-700 to 225 HU). Create a "Cancellous Bone" mask by boolean subtraction of Cortical Bone from Full Bone.

3D Reconstruction: Generate 3D surfaces for Cortical Bone and Soft Tissue. Smooth the Cortical Bone surface with at least 5 iterations and decimate it below 300,000 triangles. Set the Soft Tissue surface semi-transparent so the bone is visible underneath.

Measurements: Place at least 3 linear measurements (maximum anteroposterior diameter, maximum biparietal width, and one orbital dimension) and one angular measurement of the cranial base angle.

Documentation and Export to /home/ga/Documents/case_conference/: cortical_bone.stl (optimized bone surface), soft_tissue.ply (skin envelope surface), anterior_view.png (front-facing 3D screenshot), lateral_view.png (right lateral 3D screenshot), cross_section.png (sagittal clipping plane view revealing internal bone structure).

Save the complete project to /home/ga/Documents/case_conference/case_package.inv3.

Login credentials: username=ga, password=password123
